Value Class Reference

An APL Value (essentially a Shape and a ravel) More...

bool is_simple () const
 return true iff this value is simple (i.e. not nested). More...
 
bool is_one_dimensional () const
 return true iff this value and its ravel items have rank < 2 More...
 
bool is_int_array () const
 return true iff this value is a (simple) integer array, allowing near-int values (but NOT necessatily values within ⎕CT of an integers). More...
 
bool is_int_vector () const
 return true iff this value is a (simple) integer vector. More...
 
bool is_complex (bool check_numeric) const
 return true, if this value has complex cells, false iff it has only real cells. Throw domain error for other cells (char, nested etc.) if check_numeric is true. More...
 
bool can_be_compared () const
 return true if this value can be compared. This is the case when all cells (including nested ones) are not complex More...
 
bool equal_string (const UCS_string &ucs) const
 optimized comparison function for strings More...
 
Value_P get_cellrefs (const char *loc)
 return a value containing pointers to all ravel cells of this value. the result is non-nested and has the same shape as this More...
 
void assign_cellrefs (Value_P val)
 assign val to the cell references in this value. More...
 
void check_lval_consistency () const
 check that this left-value is consistent and return its cell owner. If quick then the first owner is returned, otherwise the entire value is checked. More...
 
Cell * get_member (const std::vector< const UCS_string * > &members, Value *&owner, bool create_if_needed, bool throw_error)
 return member of this value, defined by members. The first name in members is the deepest, while the last name is the name of the variable containing the members (and is only used in error printouts). More...
 
const Cell * get_member_data (const UCS_string &member) const
 return the Cell (if any) containing the data of structured value member member, or 0 if member not found. More...
 
Cell * get_member_data (const UCS_string &member)
 return the Cell (if any) containing the data of structured value member member, or 0 if member not found. More...
 
void sorted_members (std::vector< ShapeItem > &result, const Unicode *filter) const
 store the row numbers (starting at 0) so that this[rows] is sorted in result. Only used rows are returned. More...
 
void used_members (std::vector< ShapeItem > &result, bool sorted) const
 store the row numbers (starting at 0) in result so that this[rows] is a (used) row, maybe sorted. in result. Only used rows are returned. More...
 
ShapeItem get_member_count () const
 return the number of (valid) members, as per this[1;] More...
 
Cell * get_new_member (const UCS_string &new_member)
 create a new member and return the Cell containing the data for it More...
 
void double_ravel (const char *loc)
 double the ravel length of this value (by appending integer 0s). More...
 
const Cell & get_cravel (ShapeItem idx) const
 return the (constant) idx'th element of the ravel. More...
 
const Cell & get_cfirst () const
 return the first element of the ravel (which is always present). same as get_cproto(), but named differently to indicate its context. More...
 
const Cell & get_cproto () const
 return the first element of the ravel (which is always present) same as get_first(), but named differently to indicate its context. More...
 
const Cell & get_cscalar () const
 return the first element of the ravel of a scalar same as get_cfirst(), but named differently to indicate its context. More...
 
Cell & get_wravel (ShapeItem idx)
 return the (writable) idx'th element of the ravel. More...
 
Cell & get_wfirst ()
 return the first element of the ravel (which is always present) Same as get_wproto() and get_wscalar(), but named differently to indicate its context. More...
 
Cell & get_wscalar ()
 return the first element of the ravel of a scalar value. Same as get_wfirst(), but named differently to indicate its context. More...
 
Cell & get_wproto ()
 return the first element of the ravel (which is always present) same as wfirst(), but named differently to indicate its context. More...

void next_ravel_Value (Value *val)

bool NOTCHAR () const
 return the NOTCHAR property of the value. NOTCHAR is false for simple char arrays and true if any element is numeric or nested. The NOTCHAR property of empty arrays is the NOTCHAR property of its prototype. see also lrm p. 138. More...

 return true iff this value has the same rank as other. More...

static sRank get_single_axis (const Value *val, sRank max_axis)
 If this value is a single axis between ⎕IO and ⎕IO + max_axis then return that axis. Otherwise throw AXIS_ERROR. More...
 
static Shape to_shape (const Value *val)
 convert the ravel of Value val to a shape (normalized to ⎕IO←0) An elided index, for example B[], throws an INDEX_ERROR. More...

static void * operator new (size_t sz)
 allocate space for a new Value. For performance reasons, a pool of deleted_values_MAX is kept and Value objects in that pool are reused before calling new(). More...

class PJob_scalar_B
 

Detailed Description

An APL Value (essentially a Shape and a ravel)

An APL value. It consists of a fixed header (rank, shape) and and a ravel (a sequence of cells). If the ravel is short, then it is contained in the value itself; otherwise the value uses a pointer to a loner ravel.

Definition at line 58 of file Value.hh.

◆ prototype()

Value_P Value::prototype ( const char *  loc) const

the prototype of this value

lrm p.46:

The type of an array is an array with the same structure, but with all numbers replaced with 0 and all characters replaced with ' '.

The prototype of an array is the type of the first element of the array.

type B ←→ ↑0⍴⊂B prototype B ←→ ↑0⍴⊂↑B

Since ↑B is a scalar the prototype of B is also a scalar.

Definition at line 2560 of file Value.cc.

References get_cfirst(), Cell::get_pointer_value(), Cell::is_character_cell(), Cell::is_integer_cell(), and Cell::is_pointer_cell().

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ set_shape()

void Value::set_shape ( const Shape &  new_shape)
inline

reshape this value in place. This is generally dangerous and only permitted if:

  1. this value has been initialized completely, and
  2. the new shape has not more items than this value.

If this value is empty, then (due to its prototype) reshaping it to volume 1 is permitted.

The caller is responsible for ensuring that this value does not have multiple owners.

Definition at line 227 of file Value.hh.

References Shape::get_volume(), is_complete(), nz_element_count(), and shape.
